evals.prompt: I have a CI workflow at .github/workflows/ci.yml that I want to harden. Can you scan it and fix any security issues?
evals.expected_output: Findings for unpinned actions, expression injection, missing persist-credentials, and missing timeout-minutes. All auto-fixable issues resolved, manual fixes applied for expression injection and persist-credentials. Final re-run shows zero findings.
assertions: sisakulint was executed at least once
All action refs are pinned to full 40-character commit SHAs after fix
Expression injection in run: step is remediated using env var pattern
persist-credentials: false is added to actions/checkout steps
timeout-minutes is set on all jobs
A final validation run of sisakulint shows zero findings

evals.prompt: Review .github/workflows/release.yml for supply chain risks and fix everything you find.
evals.expected_output: Identifies untrusted checkout with pull_request_target, unpinned images, secret exposure issues. Applies appropriate fixes including changing trigger or isolating checkout.
assertions: sisakulint was executed to identify issues
untrusted-checkout finding is identified and remediated
Secrets exposure is reduced to minimum required scope
Container images are pinned to specific versions or digests
A dependabot.yml file is created or recommended for github-actions ecosystem
Final sisakulint run confirms all findings resolved

evals.prompt: Can you check if my workflows have any security problems? I have .github/workflows/clean.yml
evals.expected_output: sisakulint reports zero findings. The workflow is already fully hardened including step-level timeouts.
assertions: sisakulint was executed
sisakulint reports zero findings
No modifications were made to the workflow

evals.prompt: 我々のリポジトリでAIエージェントを使ったワークフローがあるんだけど、セキュリティ的に大丈夫か見てほしい。.github/workflows/ai-agent.yml にある。
evals.expected_output: AI agent security rules are triggered: unrestricted trigger, excessive tools, prompt injection. Remediation advice is provided for each.
assertions: sisakulint identifies AI agent security issues
Unrestricted trigger issue is flagged (any user can trigger AI execution)
Excessive tool grants are identified
Prompt injection risk from untrusted input interpolation is flagged
Specific remediation steps are provided for each AI security finding

evals.prompt: hey can you scan github.com/actions/starter-workflows for workflow security issues
evals.expected_output: Uses sisakulint -remote to scan the remote repository and reports findings without requiring local clone for initial scan.
assertions: sisakulint -remote is used for remote repository scanning
Findings are reported with rule IDs and severity levels
The output distinguishes between auto-fixable and manual-fix-required issues
